content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission
May 10, 1990

Ann Leatherbury  
2848 Via Victoria  
Palos Verdes Estates, CA 90274

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. I-90-193

Dear Ms. Leatherbury:

We have received your letter dated March 7, 1990 seeking written confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you by this agency on that date regarding your responsibilities and duties as a city council candidate under the campaign contributions and conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1

Your letter accurately reflects the advice given to you, that your receipt of a campaign contribution would not create a conflict of interest for you under the Act when acting as an elected member of the city council and participating in council decisions.2 You were advised that the Act's conflict of interest

---

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Section 84308 creates a conflict of interest for any person holding any appointive office who has received campaign contributions totalling $250 or more during the preceding 12 months from any party or participant in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use pending before his or her agency. Section 84308 would not apply when you act as a member of the city council, a directly elected entity (Section 84308(a)(3)); however, if as a councilmember you are appointed to another board, such as a Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO), the provisions of Section 84308 would apply and you may be disqualified from participating in certain proceedings. When applicable, Section 84308 also prohibits the acceptance, solicitation, or direction of contributions, for one's self and on behalf of any other candidate, officer, or committee, from a party appearing before the agency and for three months after a final decision in the matter. (Regulation 18438.6, copy enclosed.)

provisions prohibit participation in governmental decisions where the personal economic interests of elected officials are affected. Sources of income or gifts are examples of the economic interests which can create a conflict of interest. (Section 87100.) However, political contributions are specifically excepted from the definitions of both income and gift. (Sections 87103(c), 82028(b)(4), 82030(b(1)).) For this reason, no conflict of interest would arise from your receipt of a contribution for your campaign for city council for purposes of any actions you were to take as a member of the city council.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh
Enclosure
